---
title: "grow_investment"
author: "Abidur Rahman"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.


### adjust_for_inflation()

**Purpose:**  
Converts nominal money values into inflation-adjusted values.

**What it does:**  
This function reduces future values by the inflation rate over a number of years so you can compare them in today's dollars.

**Use case:**  
Use this when you want to see the real purchasing power of savings, income, or expenses.

**Inputs:**  
- `amount`: A numeric vector of money amounts.
- `inflation_rate`: Annual inflation rate as a decimal.
- `years`: Number of years in the future.

**Output:**  
A numeric vector of inflation-adjusted values.

**Example:**  
`adjust_for_inflation(c(100, 250, 500), 0.03, 5)`
